english: written grág-pa (grag pa), dga-grágs ₐúr-ba (dga' grags 'ur ba) to raise shouts of joy.

W. *daṅ-mo*, I. adj. cold, cool, gráṅ-baí gnas (grang ba'i gnas) a cool place; *daṅ-mo rag* W., *ḍhaṅ-ghi dúg* C. I am cold. II. sbst. coldness, cold, gráṅ-ba ni dró-bar gyur (grang ba ni dro bar gyur) the cold changed into warmth Dzl.: *mén-tog daṅ-mo pog* W. the cold has struck, killed, the flowers. - graṅ-nád (grang nad) the cold fit of the ague, *ḍhaṅ-ṭí* (lit. mkris (mkris)) C. id. - *daṅ-nád* W. synon. with grúm-bu (grum bu), gout, rheumatism, arthritic pain; graṅ-dro (grang dro) cold and warmth, graṅ-dro-méd-pai rás-kyaṅ ₐdi (grang dro med pa'i ras kyang 'di) this thin cloth which constitutes my clothing, in warm and in cold weather Mil., v. méd-pa (med pa); also warmth in a relative sense, temperature. - graṅ-ṡúm (grang shum) Lt., graṅ-ṡúm byéd-pa (grang shum byed pa) to shiver with cold Schr. III. vb., also graṅs-pa (grangs pa) 1. to get or grow cold, gráṅs-su bcùg-pa (grangs su bcug pa) Lex. to let grow cold; graṅs gró-bar ₐdug (grangs gro bar 'dug) it will grow cold Mil.; graṅ mi bya (grang mi bya) one must not suffer (the child) to catch cold Lt. - 2. to count, judge, consider, v. bgráṅ-ba (bgrang ba); also Zam.: cès gráṅ-naaṅ (ces grang na'ang) though such may be supposed; Cs. and Schr. have also graṅ (grang) perhaps, yin graṅ (yin grang) perhaps it may be so.

col. also *daṅ-ka*, Ssk. संख्या number, frq., lan graṅs-dú-mar (lan grangs du mar) a number of times Mil.; graṅs-méd-pa (grangs med pa), eleg. graṅs-ma-mcíṡ-pa (grangs ma mchis pa) innumerable; gráṅs-cán (grangs can) numerous (?) Cs.; gráṅs-cán-pa (grangs can pa) the atheistic Sankhya sect of the Brahmans (Ban. p. 66); *dá-daṅ zág-daṅ gyáb-ce* W. to date (lit. to write down the number of month and day); graṅs ₐdébs-pa (grangs 'debs pa) or rtsí-ba (rtsi ba) to count Cs. - graṅs-brdá (grangs brda) (Cs. Gram. § 235) symbolical numerals, certain nouns, which in some books are used instead of the usual numerals, e.g. mig (mig), eye, for 'two'.
to grow cold, v. gráṅ-ba (grang ba) III.

1. preparation, arrangements, measures; a contrivance, grabs byéd-pa (grabs byed pa) to make preparations for, to be on the point of, frq., ₐgró-bai grabs byéd-pa ('gro ba'i grabs byed pa), to make preparations for departing, gsód-grabs yód-pai tsé-na (gsod grabs yod pa'i tse na) just as preparations were made for slaughtering them Mil.; *ko kyug dhab jhe* C. he is getting sick, is going to vomit; káb-grabs (khab grabs), ₐdzíṅ-grabs ('dzing grabs) the making one's self ready for combat. - 2. col. also for gros (gros), deliberation, *ne čir dhab jhe dug* C. they are deliberating about me; *naṅ-náṅ-ñi dabs tún-ne* W. on mutual agreement.
1. swamp, marsh, fen Lex. - 2. grém-pa (grem pa) Mng.

Ssk. पङ्क्ति 1. row, series, class, esp. a row of persons, gral(-du) sgrig-pa (gral (-du) sgrig pa) to order, to dispose in rows, in rank and file; grál-gyi tóg-ma (gral gyi tog ma), ltag (ltag), goṅ (gong), more frq. gral-mgó (gral mgo) the upper end of a row, the uppermost place, the seat at the head of the table; t'á-ma (tha ma), ₐog ('og) or gral-mjúg(-rjug) (gral mjug (-rjug)) the lower end; gral mgó-ma (gral mgo ma) the first, the head person Mil.; gyas-grál (g.yas gral) the right-hand end, gyon-grál (g.yon gral) the left-hand end; gral-rím (gral rim) C. claim, title, rgan-gźon-gral-rím (rgan gzhon gral rim) the right of seniority; grál-pa (gral pa) a beer-house customer; gral-gtám (gral gtam) tap-house talk Mil.; dbaṅ-grál (dbang gral) the row of supplicants for a benediction; mčed-grógs daṅ dbaṅ-grál mtún dús-su (mched grogs dang dbang gral mthun dus su) Mil. if you sit with your fellow-believers in one row, on one mat; *če-ḍál-la čud soṅ* W. he has entered into the row, the class, of adults. - 2. bench. - 3. proportionality (?), *źeṅ-ríṅ ḍal-méd daṅ* W. with his disproportioned length and breadth, his unwieldiness. - 4. *mi źig-la ḍal źig ḍig-cè* W. (lit. sgrig-pa (sgrig pa)) W. to play a trick to a person.
a small beam, rafter, Cs.; grál-bu (gral bu), gral-pyám (gral phyam) S.g. roof-laths, sticks which are laid close together and covered with earth.
class, order, series; rank, dignity; tribe Cs.
1. for drás-pa (dras pa). - 2. to bind, v. grágs-pa (grags pa).

(so pronounced in Pur.) 1. knife, grís ₐcòd-pa (gris 'cod pa), *ḍi daṅ ċád-ce* W., to cut with a knife, but also grír rnám-pa (grir rnam pa), gsód-pa (gsod pa), gúm-pa (gum pa) Ma: to kill with a knife;
